participant_local_context: nara_7 is present for the visible event or receives it through local supervisor/worker channels.
The participant has access only to observed action, speech, role knowledge, current scene state, and established local relationships.
observable_event_summary: Lio plants one boot at the Kappa-7 threshold line, lifts his scheduler slate where the port camera can see it, and enters a freeze note without stepping into the artery. Clearance freeze accepted. Logging Kappa-7 as no-entry pending seal, air, thermal reconciliation and baseline anchor verification. Dax, give me two anchors on the rail-side check only, eyes and instruments, no bodies past the threshold. Nara-7, state the sequence exactly as you read it. I want the False-Seam pattern named in the maintenance log, not shaved down into 'delay pending review.'
known_facts: Kappa-7 is a dangerous service artery with live seal-air-thermal consequences.
The event is visible enough to change shop-floor expectations.
Actor intent is not directly known.
active_pressures: Preserve or challenge authority without losing the room.
Decide whether the incident means safety, malfunction, labor action, or liability.
perceived_relationships: Existing Pallas agent-state relationship stances apply, but this appraisal records only the participant local read after this event.
interpretation: Nara reads Lio as preserving her hazard sequence in the official log without making her stand alone as the cause.
confidence_notes: Manual pilot appraisal. Confidence is qualitative because the appraiser is currently coordinator-reviewed rather than model-produced.
